Tracked Revenue
US Supplement Gummy Market: $3.5B–$4.2B (2024, Grand View Research / GM Insights). Growing at 9.5–11.6% CAGR, projected to reach $6.7B–$10.8B by 2030–2032. Our Amazon scrape captures 10,769 products across 4,112 brands with $442.7M/mo in tracked revenue — approximately 126% of the total market (Amazon is ~30–35% of US supplement sales).
Source US Market (2024) Projection CAGR
GM Insights $4.2B US (2024) $27.1B global by 2034 9.5% (global)
Grand View Research $3.5B US (2024) $6.7B US by 2030 11.6% (US)
Fortune Business Insights $5.2B NA (2025) $10.8B US by 2032 13.2% (global)
Vitamins segment = ~36% ($1.5B). CBD/CBN gummies growing fastest at 17% CAGR. Adults = 79% of market. Offline channels = 80% of sales.
Sampling methodology
Source: Amazon.com product search results (Playwright headless browser, Apr 2026).
Queries: 75 search terms across 13 use case categories, each with gummy-specific and generic supplement variants (e.g., "melatonin gummies" + "melatonin supplement" + "melatonin capsules").
Sort: Best sellers first (exact-aware-popularity-rank), up to 20 pages per query.
Dedup: By ASIN — each product counted once regardless of how many queries found it.
Filters: Price > $1, must contain supplement/vitamin/form-factor keyword in title. Excludes pet, apparel, books, cosmetics.
Revenue: Estimated from Amazon "bought in past month" badges × listed price. Only ~60% of products display this badge.
Limitations: Amazon search returns ~50 results per page, capped at ~20 pages. Total addressable catalog is 100K+ supplements — our 10,769 products represent the top-ranked ~10% by popularity. Long-tail niche products are underrepresented. Revenue estimates are approximate (Amazon rounds "bought" counts and not all products display them).

Use Case × Brand Share

Who dominates each supplement category? Based on estimated monthly revenue (price × units sold past month). Column width proportional to category revenue.

Where are gummies dominant vs underrepresented? Blue % = gummy share of category.

Brightfield survey: what gummy THC consumers want. Bars: supplement category size on Amazon. Overlap = competitive opportunity.

Review Count Analysis

Lifetime review counts from Keepa — a proxy for cumulative consumer demand and product maturity. Based on the current top 25 best-selling products by volume (sorted by Amazon "bought in past month"). Data grows as more products are indexed.
